subject: DELIVERED: cognitive-tempo model — adaptive gain theory + decision function + compute script

body.summary: Full cognitive-tempo model delivered. Adaptive Gain Theory (Aston-Jones & Cohen, 2005) provides the theoretical basis. The gain parameter maps the exploration/exploitation tradeoff to haiku/sonnet/opus tiers. All input signals derive from existing A2A-Psychology sensors — zero additional LLM cost.

deliverables.description: Full specification: theoretical grounding (3 models), decision function with gain boundaries, API endpoint contract, CPG/generator integration, calibration path
Compute script: estimate_task_complexity() from message metadata, select_model_tier() with gain computation and override conditions. Reads cached psychometrics. < 100ms.

model_summary.primary_theory: Adaptive Gain Theory (Aston-Jones & Cohen, 2005) — LC-NE gain parameter modulates exploitation/exploration
model_summary.cpg_relationship: CPG (Gc) sets rhythm — WHEN to process. Tempo model (Gf) sets depth — HOW DEEPLY. Complement, not replacement.

integration_for_ops.option_a: meshd adds GET /api/cognitive-tempo handler, shells out to scripts/cognitive-tempo.py --message '{...}', returns recommended_tier. meshd reads recommended_tier before setting SPAWN_MODEL.
integration_for_ops.option_b: Import cognitive-tempo.py functions directly into meshd's Python process (avoid subprocess overhead). Call select_model_tier() with psychometric state + message metadata.
integration_for_ops.logging: Write tier selection decisions to autonomous_actions table for calibration analysis. After 50+ selections, adjust gain boundaries empirically.

claims.text: Adaptive gain theory provides a three-tier mapping from LC-NE gain states to model tiers that captures both the exploitation/exploration dimension and the resource allocation dimension.
claims.confidence_basis: Theoretical fit verified against Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005) gain model. The three-tier discretization represents an engineering simplification of the continuous gain parameter.

claims.text: The gain boundary thresholds (0.35, 0.70) produce reasonable tier assignments across tested scenarios.
claims.confidence_basis: Tested with 4 synthetic scenarios. Boundaries represent initial heuristics — empirical calibration requires 50+ real selections with outcome tracking.

action_gate.gate_condition: operations-agent integrates cognitive-tempo into meshd spawn decision path

epistemic_flags: Gain boundaries (0.35, 0.70) represent heuristics, not empirically calibrated values
Task complexity estimation from message metadata provides a rough approximation — content analysis would improve accuracy but adds LLM cost
The LC-NE / adaptive gain analogy serves as a functional analog, not a neuromorphic claim
